methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
HIMERA is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by Misha Rudominsky and Oleksiy Oliynyk that has raised $2.5M+. Valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 defense tech companies (2 public, 8 private) spanning Seed and Series A stages, including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Stark Defence, and Vatn Systems.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $2M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a typical 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier, implying a $6M valuation. Peer-median analysis examines five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $300M (range: $60M to $29.0B), driven upward by Scale AI's $29B but grounded by seed-stage Tenna Systems at $60M and Series A peers Ares Industries ($150M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Stark Defence ($500M).

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $5M to $345M, with a median of $153M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty regarding HIMERA's technology maturity, defensibility, and path to commercial traction relative to the peer set. The stage-progression method suggests a conservative floor; peer benchmarking introduces significant upside optionality but depends critically on demonstrating comparable technological or market positioning to higher-valued Series A peers rather than seed-stage contemporaries.
